title: AMC Entertainment Beats Street On Strong 4Q Movie Slate, Stock Pops, CEO Sees 'Material Growth' In 2025 And Beyond
text: AMC Entertainment beat Wall Street expectations on strong fourth quarter movie slate results. More than 62 million guests visited AMC theaters worldwide in the fourth quarter of 2024, marking a post-pandemic fourth quarter record and a healthy 20% increase compared to 2023. AMC believes the 2025 domestic industry box office should easily surpass that of 2024, with bankable titles hitting theaters next year, such as Paramount's Mission: Impossible 8, Universal's Jurassic World 4, and Disney titles. CEO Adam Aron admitted that domestic theater attendance is down almost 40% from pre-pandemic 2019 levels, indicating that AMC's recovery journey remains ongoing. The stock popped following the earnings announcement as CEO Aron projected material growth in 2025 and beyond driven by improved box office performance and operational efficiency.
